facebook.content_angle: The race start is the most under-trained 2 seconds in competitive swimming — track start position, reaction time, and a clean underwater phase can save 0.5+ seconds before the first stroke is even taken
facebook.post_text:
🎯 The race is already decided before you hit the water — and most swimmers don't know it.

If you're grinding through hours of lap training but never drilling your start, you could be giving away 0.5+ seconds before your first stroke is even taken.

Here's what the data actually says:
👉 Elite reaction time sits at 0.16–0.17 seconds — the difference between a podium and nowhere
👉 The track start (one foot forward, one back) delivers more forward power and stability than a grab start
👉 **The underwater phase — up to 15 metres — is where races are won.** Most club swimmers resurface far too early.

A biomechanics review of Olympic data found that a 0.1-second improvement in start time would have changed **65 medals** across 32 years of competition. 😳

Here's your 3-step start fix for this week:
✔️ Drill the track start: back foot on the wedge, front toes gripping the edge — explode *forward*, not up
✔️ Enter tight and hold your streamline until at least 10 metres before breaking out
✔️ Jaked's compression fabric holds your streamline shape longer underwater — free speed at every single start

Are you training your start — or leaving it to chance on race day? 👇
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop a ⚡ if you actually drill your starts — or tag the teammate who never practises off the blocks! 👇
